sections.paragraph:
κοκκύζω (κόκκυ ‘the cry of a cock’; Hes. et al.; TestSol 9:3 C) to crow of a cock (Cratinus et al.; Plato Com. [V/IV b.c.] 209 p. 659 K.; Hyperid., Fgm. 239) gospel fragment fr. Fayum, restored (Kl. T. 83, p. 23, 10=Otero [after Harnack] I 85 Fgm. ln. 7 [lit.]=ASyn. 315, 53; cp. Mk 14:30).—DELG s.v. κόκκυ.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Com. Com. = Comicorum Atticorum fragmenta (TKock) and related collections of comedy fragments as noted in text; various dates—List 5

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. fr. = from

Kl. T. Kl. T. = Kleine Texte, ed. HLietzmann, also KAland—List 6

Otero Otero = AdeSantosO., Los Evangelios Apocrifos—List 5

  1. ln. = line

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 554
